mnemonics: CONNECTedness: Can't Operate Nicely if Not Every Component is Together & Entirely Detected
keyPoints: A metric space is connected if it cannot be represented as the union of two disjoint non-empty open sets.
Connectedness is a topological property, preserved under continuous functions.
The continuous image of a connected set is connected.
commonMistakes: Confusing a space being connected with it being path-connected; path-connected implies connected, but the converse is not always true.
Assuming that if a set is not open, it cannot be disconnected; connectedness is defined using open sets, but the disconnected sets themselves do not need to be open.
